summary: Chest pain associated with unstable angina often happens without exercise; thus, occurring at rest characterizes its variability and severity more appropriately than fixed patterns like lasting up to ten minutes or resolving upon cessation as seen in conditions such as stable angina. The most accurate description among choices matches spontaneous occurrence fittingly under instability context.
